mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward rank IC of UNRATE changes is above +0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.5%, it signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle that increases the discount rate on equity cash flows and depresses valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than half of the triggered instances, or the average excess return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: The joint decline in housing starts below their 12-month moving average and a rising federal funds rate indicates a weakening housing market under monetary tightening, which historically foreshadows economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where both conditions hold, or the average excess return is not significantly negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, the erosion of real purchasing power and anticipation of tighter monetary policy compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than half of the triggered instances, or the average excess return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output, robust economic activity, and improving corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward rank IC of INDPRO changes is below -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative returns occur in more than 60% of periods with rising IP, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly retail sales growth exceeding 0.5% signals strong consumer spending that boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to the data.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns are negative in more than half of the triggered instances, or the average excess return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is rising while nonfarm payrolls remain above their 10-year moving average, the economy is in a late-cycle stage where labor market tightness coexists with monetary tightening, historically preceding economic contraction and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over 3 months and PAYEMS > 10-year MA
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average excess return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts decline more than 10% year-over-year, it signals a severe downturn in the housing market that reduces construction activity, consumer wealth, and bank lending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than half of the triggered instances, or the average excess return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), reject.
